title: 10 Effective Herbal Teas for Nasal Congestion and Cold Relief
intro_desc: Herbal teas for nasal congestion are a natural remedy that helps alleviate nasal blockages and congestion by loosening mucus and reducing inflammation. These teas are made from various herbs, including Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Thymus vulgaris, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Rosmarinus officinalis, Hyptis suaveolens, Sambucus nigra, Lavandula angustifolia, and Mentha x piperita. If left untreated, nasal congestion can lead to difficulty breathing, headaches, fatigue, and sleep disturbances, impacting daily activities and overall quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tea is a popular remedy for nasal congestion because of its powerful decongestant properties. The main active compound in eucalyptus, called eucalyptol, helps to break down and clear mucus from the nasal passages. When you inhale the steam from the tea, the eucalyptol molecules travel up your nose and reach the thin layer of mucus that's causing congestion. The eucalyptol then dissolves the mucus, making it easier to breathe and reducing the feeling of stuffiness in your nose. As the mucus is cleared, the nasal passages are able to function more efficiently, allowing air to flow in and out of the lungs more easily. This can provide quick relief from nasal congestion caused by colds, allergies, and sinus infections. The anti-inflammatory properties of eucalyptus globulus tea also help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, further opening up airways and improving breathing.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for nasal congestion because it helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion caused by colds and allergies.
Eucalyptol, found in peppermint oil, helps relieve nasal congestion because it acts as a natural expectorant, loosening and thinning mucus in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Cineole is good for nasal congestion because it has natural decongestant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe.
Tannins are good for nasal congestion because they help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, loosen mucus, and ease congestion by their natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.

Polyphenols, also known as Peppermint, are good for nasal congestion because they have natural decongestant properties that help ease breathing by reducing inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.

remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for nasal congestion
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Mentha x piperita herbal tea that helps with nasal congestion, start by gathering a few sprigs of fresh peppermint leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried peppermint. Next, boil 1 cup of water in a pot, then remove it from the heat source. Add the peppermint leaves or dried peppermint to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the menthol and menthone in the peppermint to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the peppermint leaves. Add honey to taste if desired, as the menthol in peppermint can be quite strong. After the tea has cooled slightly, inhale the vapor to help clear nasal congestion, or drink the tea to help soothe and calm the respiratory system.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a popular remedy for nasal congestion due to its natural decongestant properties. The tea contains compounds that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing. When we have a cold or allergies, our body produces excess mucus to trap germs and other foreign particles. However, this excess mucus can cause congestion and difficulty breathing through the nose. Echinacea purpurea tea helps to thin out this excess mucus, making it easier to expel and relieve congestion. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce the severity of cold and allergy symptoms, such as sneezing, coughing, and sinus pressure. By drinking Echinacea purpurea tea, people can experience relief from nasal congestion and breathe more easily. The tea also has antimicrobial properties, which can help to fight off infections and promote a faster recovery from illness. By reducing inflammation and promoting drainage, Echinacea purpurea tea can provide quick and effective relief from nasal congestion.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for nasal congestion because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and improve airflow, making it easier to breathe.

Alkylamides, also known as peppermint, are good for nasal congestion because they help to open up airways by releasing a natural chemical called eucalyptol, which reduces swelling in the nasal passages and makes it easier to breathe.
Kaempferol is good for nasal congestion because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and open airways in the nose, making it easier to breathe.
Cichoric acid is good for nasal congestion because it helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for nasal congestion
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Mentha x piperita herbal teas that help with nasal congestion, start by gathering a few sprigs of fresh peppermint leaves. You can either grow your own peppermint plant in your garden or buy it from a local health store. Next, boil a cup of water and let it cool for a minute or two. Then, add a few sprigs of peppermint leaves to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. As the leaves infuse, the peppermint oil will be released into the water, creating a soothing and refreshing tea. Once the tea has steeped, strain it into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth. You can add a bit of honey to sweeten the tea if you like, but it's not necessary. Finally, drink the tea hot or iced, depending on your preference. The menthol in peppermint can help open up your airways and ease nasal congestion, making it a great natural remedy for a stuffy nose.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a natural remedy for nasal congestion. The active compound in ginger, called gingerol, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages. When you have a cold or allergies, your nasal passages can become inflamed and swollen, making it hard to breathe. Ginger tea helps to ease this inflammation, making it easier to breathe and clear out mucus. Additionally, ginger has natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear out mucus from your lungs and nasal passages. Drinking ginger tea can help to ease congestion and sinus pressure, making it a popular remedy for people who suffer from colds, allergies, and sinus infections.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for nasal congestion because they help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Shogaols are good for nasal congestion because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and relax muscles in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing.
Zingiberene is good for nasal congestion because it acts as a natural decongestant that helps to open up airways and ease breathing by reducing inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for nasal congestion
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Mentha x piperita herbal teas that help with nasal congestion, start by gathering a few sprigs of fresh peppermint leaves, also known as Mentha x piperita. You can find these leaves in your garden or purchase them from a local health food store. Next, place the leaves in a large pot and add 2 cups of boiling water. Let the leaves steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the oils and flavors to release into the water. You can also add a few sprigs of eucalyptus leaves if you have them, as they complement the peppermint flavor and help open up airways. Once the tea has steeped, strain the leaves from the water and let it cool slightly. Pour the tea into a cup and add a squeeze of honey to taste. Drink the tea hot or warm, ideally when you feel a cold or sinus infection coming on, as the menthol and menthone in the peppermint leaves help ease congestion and provide relief from nasal congestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tea is a natural remedy for nasal congestion. The main reason is its expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages. The compounds in Thymus vulgaris, such as thymol and carvacrol, have anti-inflammatory effects that reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. When you drink Thymus vulgaris tea, the warmth and moisture of the liquid help to thin out the mucus, allowing it to be expelled more easily. Additionally, the tea's antiseptic properties help fight off any underlying infections that may be causing the nasal congestion. This makes Thymus vulgaris tea a popular natural remedy for colds, sinusitis, and other respiratory issues. By promoting drainage and reducing inflammation, Thymus vulgaris tea can provide quick relief from nasal congestion.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for nasal congestion because it has natural decongestant properties that help to break down mucus and reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Rosmarinic acid is good for nasal congestion because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Carvacrol, also known as Peppermint, is good for nasal congestion because it helps to open up airways and reduce inflammation by thinning mucus and making it easier to breathe.

remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal teas for nasal congestion
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Mentha x piperita herbal tea that helps with nasal congestion, start by gathering a handful of fresh peppermint leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried peppermint. You can find peppermint leaves at your local health food store or grow them in your garden. Next, boil 1 cup of water in a pot and let it cool for a minute. Add the peppermint leaves or dried peppermint to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has turned a light green color and the aroma of peppermint is strong. Strain the liquid into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to catch the peppermint leaves. You can add honey to taste, if desired, to sweeten the tea. Drink the tea hot or iced, as you prefer, and the menthol in the peppermint is expected to help ease nasal congestion and open up your airways, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis tea, also known as rosemary tea, can help with nasal congestion. When we breathe in, the air enters our nostrils and gets into our body. But sometimes, our nasal passages become inflamed and swollen, making it hard to breathe. Rosemary tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in our nasal passages. This makes it easier for air to pass through, relieving congestion. Additionally, rosemary tea has natural decongestant properties that can help loosen and clear out excess mucus from our nasal passages, further relieving congestion. When we drink rosemary tea, the anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties are absorbed into our body, helping to reduce nasal congestion and make it easier to breathe.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid in <built-in method capitalize of str object at 0x76fa7655ad70> Mentha x piperita is good for nasal congestion because it helps to reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria that cause congestion and sinus infections.
Carnosic acid, or peppermint, is good for nasal congestion because it helps to thin and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe.
Tannins help reduce nasal congestion by shrinking swollen blood vessels in the nose, allowing for easier breathing and relieving stuffiness.

remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for nasal congestion
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Mentha x piperita herbal tea for nasal congestion, start by gathering a handful of fresh peppermint leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried peppermint. If you're using fresh leaves, simply bruise them gently with a spoon to release their oils. Next, boil 1 cup of water and then pour it over the peppermint, making sure the leaves are fully submerged. If using dried peppermint, you can add it directly to the boiling water. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, which will help the peppermint's oils infuse into the water. After the steeping time has passed, carefully pour the tea through a strainer into a cup to separate the leaves from the liquid. You can now drink the tea as is or add a bit of honey to taste. To get the most out of the tea, it's recommended to drink it while it's still warm, ideally 2-3 times a day to help alleviate nasal congestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea is good for nasal congestion because it helps to open up airways in the nose and sinuses. The tea contains compounds that reduce inflammation and swelling, which can block airways and cause congestion. When you drink Ginkgo biloba tea, the anti-inflammatory properties help to ease swelling and calm the mucous membranes in your nose. This makes it easier to breathe and helps to clear out mucus that's causing congestion. The tea also has a natural ability to thin out mucus, making it less sticky and easier to cough out. As a result, you may find that you can breathe more easily and feel more comfortable when you have a cold or allergies.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for nasal congestion because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.

Kaempferol is good for nasal congestion because it helps to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, relaxes the airways, and prevents the release of chemical messengers that cause blood vessels to swell.
Isorhapontigenin (peppermint oil) is good for nasal congestion because it opens up air passages in the nose by reducing swelling and inflammation, making it easier to breathe.

Ginkgolide B is good for nasal congestion because it works by blocking the action of inflammatory chemicals in the body, specifically leukotrienes, which cause the blood vessels in the nose to swell and lead to congestion.

remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for nasal congestion
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Mentha x piperita herbal teas that help with nasal congestion, start by gathering fresh or dried peppermint leaves. If using fresh leaves, simply chop them finely and set aside. If using dried leaves, you can skip this step. Next, boil a pot of water, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Add a handful of chopped fresh peppermint leaves or one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves to the pot. Steep the leaves for 5-7 minutes, allowing the aroma and flavor to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the leaves. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be careful not to add too much, as peppermint can be quite strong. Once the tea has cooled slightly, drink it to help clear your nasal passages and relieve congestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is a popular remedy for nasal congestion. This is because the tea contains compounds that help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages. Glycyrrhizin, a key component of licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties that can ease congestion and soothe a sore throat. When we drink the tea, the compounds are absorbed into the body and can help to thin out mucus, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, licorice root tea may also help to calm the airways, reducing coughing and sneezing that often accompany nasal congestion. As the tea works to clear out congestion, it can also help to ease sinus pressure and promote a sense of relief and comfort.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for nasal congestion because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and open up airways, making it easier to breathe.

Polyphenols, also known as peppermint oil, are good for nasal congestion because they help open up airways by releasing a natural decongestant that reduces swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing.
Flavonoids, also known as Peppermint, are good for nasal congestion because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and open up airways, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for nasal congestion
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint herbal tea, that helps with nasal congestion, you will need fresh peppermint leaves. Start by washing the leaves with clean water to remove any dirt or impurities. Next, chop the leaves into smaller pieces and place them in a pot of boiling water. The general rule is to use one tablespoon of peppermint leaves for every cup of water. Allow the leaves to steep in the hot water for 5-7 minutes, or until the water has reduced slightly and the flavor has been released. You can then strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the leaves. To make the tea more effective in relieving nasal congestion, you can add a squeeze of fresh lemon juice and a bit of honey to taste. Once ready, you can drink the peppermint tea hot or iced, whichever you prefer, to help ease your nasal congestion and clear your sinuses.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra tea is made from the flowers of the elder tree. The flowers contain a group of compounds called flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation in the body. When you have nasal congestion, the blood vessels in your nose become swollen, making it hard to breathe. The flavonoids in Sambucus nigra tea help calm down these swollen blood vessels, making it easier for air to pass through your nose. This can help relieve the stuffiness and congestion in your nose, making it easier to breathe. The tea also contains a compound called sambunigrin, which is a natural decongestant that helps break down mucus and relieve congestion. This makes Sambucus nigra tea a good choice for people who are struggling with nasal congestion due to colds, allergies, or sinus infections.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for nasal congestion because it helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe by opening up airways.
Tannins, also known as peppermint, are good for nasal congestion because they help open up airways by reducing inflammation and thinning out mucus, making it easier to breathe.
Isorhapontigenin (Peppermint) is good for nasal congestion because it helps open up air passages in the nose by reducing inflammation and swelling, allowing for easier breathing.

Polyphenols, also known as peppermint, are good for nasal congestion because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Kaempferol is good for nasal congestion because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and promote easy breathing.

remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal teas for nasal congestion
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint tea, that helps with nasal congestion, start by gathering fresh or dried peppermint leaves. If you have fresh leaves, choose the ones that are free from any signs of decay and wash them gently with water. Next, combine one tablespoon of peppermint leaves with one cup of boiling water in a teapot. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the leaves to release their flavor and medicinal properties. After the steeping time has passed, strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the peppermint leaves. You can now drink the peppermint tea as it is or add a sweetener like honey to taste. As you drink the tea, the menthol content in the peppermint will help to ease your nasal congestion by thinning out mucus and making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tea, also known as English lavender tea, is good for nasal congestion because of its decongestant properties. The tea contains a compound called linalool, which helps to reduce swelling in the nasal passages. When you have a cold or allergies, your nasal passages can become inflamed, causing congestion. Linalool in the tea calms the inflammation, making it easier to breathe. The tea also contains a compound called linalyl acetate, which helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel. This helps to clear out the nasal passages, relieving congestion and promoting easier breathing. Drinking the tea can also help to reduce stress and promote a good night's sleep, which can help to alleviate congestion caused by a cold or allergies.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for nasal congestion because it helps to relax muscles and open airways, reducing swelling and making it easier to breathe.
Linalyl acetate is good for nasal congestion because it has natural decongestant properties that help to relax and open airways, reducing swelling and congestion in the nose.
Roserinic acid is good for nasal congestion because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for nasal congestion
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Mentha x piperita, or peppermint herbal tea, that helps with nasal congestion, start by gathering fresh peppermint leaves or using dried peppermint herb. If using fresh leaves, choose ones with vibrant green color and no signs of wilting. Wash the leaves in cold water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, boil water in a kettle or on the stovetop, then pour it over the peppermint leaves in a teapot or a large mug. If using dried peppermint, follow the package instructions for the correct amount to use. Allow the peppermint to steep in the hot water for 5-7 minutes, which will help release its natural oils and medicinal properties. Once the tea has steeped, strain it to remove the peppermint leaves, and discard the solids. You can then add honey to sweeten the tea if desired. Drinking this tea can help ease nasal congestion and provide relief from cold symptoms. To enjoy the tea, sip it slowly while it's still warm, and feel the refreshing and cooling effects of the peppermint.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tea, also known as peppermint tea, is a popular remedy for nasal congestion. The main reason it works is because of its active ingredient, menthol. When you drink peppermint tea, the menthol is released into your body and it helps to thin out mucus. This makes it easier for your body to get rid of the congestion. The menthol also has a cooling effect on your nasal passages, which can help to reduce inflammation and swelling. As a result, your airways become clearer, and you can breathe more easily. Additionally, peppermint tea has natural decongestant properties, which can help to break down and clear out excess mucus and phlegm. When you drink peppermint tea, the menthol is absorbed into your bloodstream and travels to your lungs, where it can help to ease congestion and promote easier breathing. This makes peppermint tea a natural and effective way to relieve nasal congestion and get some much-needed relief.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for nasal congestion because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe.
Menthol is good for nasal congestion because it helps to open up airways by reducing inflammation and thinning out mucus, making it easier to breathe.
Luteolin is good for nasal congestion because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the nose, allowing mucus to drain out and making breathing easier.

remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal teas for nasal congestion
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Mentha x piperita, or peppermint, herbal tea to help with nasal congestion, you will need fresh peppermint leaves, a pot, a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth, and a cup. First, choose fresh peppermint leaves with no signs of wilting or damage. Next, boil some water in the pot and let it cool slightly. Then, place the fresh peppermint leaves in the tea infuser or cheesecloth and steep them in the hot water for 5-7 minutes, depending on how strong you like your tea. After the steeping time, remove the peppermint leaves and strain the tea into the cup if using cheesecloth. You can now drink the tea as it is, or add honey and lemon to taste. When you drink the tea, the menthol in the peppermint will help to break down the mucus in your nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.

supplementary_best: To remedy nasal congestion, a blend of Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus vulgaris, and Mentha x piperita teas is the most effective. Eucalyptus globulus tea helps loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages due to its expectorant properties. Thymus vulgaris, or thyme, has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce swelling in the nasal area and combat underlying infections. Mentha x piperita, or peppermint, has a decongestant effect that helps ease congestion by thinning mucus and promoting its expulsion. This combination works well together because Eucalyptus globulus and Thymus vulgaris target the underlying causes of nasal congestion, while Mentha x piperita provides quick relief from its symptoms. Drinking this blend of teas can provide fast and effective relief from nasal congestion by addressing its root causes.
supplementary_avoid: If you have nasal congestion, it's best to avoid herbal teas that can make the problem worse. For example, teas made from Yerba Mate (Ilex paraguariensis) and Guayusa (Ilex guayusa) may be too stimulating and dry out your mucous membranes, making congestion worse. Sage tea (Salvia officinalis) and Hyssop tea (Hyssopus officinalis) may also be problematic, as they can dry out your nasal passages and exacerbate congestion. Additionally, teas made from Black Pepper (Piper nigrum) and Cayenne Pepper (Capsicum annuum) may be too spicy and irritate your nasal passages, making congestion worse. Finally, teas made from Licorice Root (Glycyrrhiza uralensis) should be avoided, as it can raise blood pressure and worsen congestion in people with pre-existing conditions.
supplementary_other_remedies: When nasal congestion strikes, there are several natural remedies that can help ease the discomfort. Eucalyptus oil, for example, is a popular remedy that can be inhaled to relieve congestion. The oil is added to a diffuser or a warm bath, releasing a soothing aroma that can help open up airways. This is because eucalyptus oil contains compounds that break down and thin mucus, making it easier to breathe.  Another natural remedy is steam inhalation, which can be achieved by taking a hot shower or inhaling steam from a bowl of hot water. This helps to loosen and clear out mucus, reducing congestion. The warmth of the steam also helps to relax the nasal passages, allowing air to flow more freely.  Some people find relief from nasal congestion by using a neti pot, a small device that rinses the nasal passages with a saline solution. The solution helps to flush out mucus and debris, reducing congestion and promoting healthy nasal function.  Nasal strips are also a natural remedy for nasal congestion. They are small adhesive strips that are placed on the bridge of the nose, helping to open up the airways and improve breathing.  Nasal irrigation with a saline solution is another effective natural remedy for nasal congestion. This involves using a device to rinse the nasal passages with a saltwater solution, which helps to clear out mucus and debris.  Saline nasal sprays can also be used to help relieve nasal congestion. These sprays help to moisturize the nasal passages and loosen mucus, making it easier to breathe.  Elevating the head while sleeping can also help to relieve nasal congestion. This is because gravity helps to reduce congestion by allowing mucus to drain out of the nasal passages more easily.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with nasal congestion often experience other ailments due to the interconnectedness of the body's systems. One common issue is headaches, caused by the buildup of mucus and pressure in the sinuses. Eucalyptus oil, found in herbs like eucalyptus and peppermint, helps to clear airways and reduce inflammation, which can ease headache symptoms.  Another related problem is sore throats, often caused by postnasal drip, where mucus runs down the back of the throat. Slipping thistle and licorice root herbs have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect the throat, reducing discomfort.  Additionally, people with nasal congestion may experience coughing, as the body tries to clear out mucus. Wild indigo and ginkgo biloba herbs have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and remove mucus, reducing coughing.  Sinus infections are also common, caused by bacterial or viral infections. Goldenseal and echinacea herbs have antimicrobial properties, helping to fight off infections and reduce sinus pressure.  Finally, fatigue and difficulty sleeping may occur due to nasal congestion. Ashwagandha and chamomile herbs have calming and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce stress and promote restful sleep.
